message:
feat(conformance): add raptorq artifact verification suite and gate binary

Add a comprehensive raptorq artifact verification suite that validates the
integrity and freshness of committed durability artifacts (sidecars, scrub
reports, and decode proofs) for both the conformance and benchmark bundles.

New raptorq artifact suite (`run_raptorq_artifact_suite`):
- Defines `BundleArtifactSpec` structs mapping each bundle ID to its source
  files and expected artifact paths on disk.
- Verifies that all source files and artifact files exist.
- Parses and validates sidecar, scrub report, and decode proof JSON artifacts
  for correct schema_version (must be 1) and matching bundle_id.
- Rebuilds the bundle payload from source files and computes a fresh SHA-256
  hash, then cross-checks it against the source_hash in the sidecar, the
  expected/decoded hashes in the scrub report, and the expected/recovered
  hashes in the decode proof.
- Validates sidecar internal consistency (total_symbols matches symbols array
  length, source_size matches actual payload byte length).
- Performs staleness detection by comparing source file mtimes against artifact
  mtimes and generated_at_unix_ms timestamps, gated behind a hash-match
  fallback to avoid false positives when artifacts are regenerated.
- Confirms scrub status is "ok" with both full_decode_match and
  recovery_decode_match flags true, and decode proof recovery_success is true.

Deterministic payload hashing fix in `build_bundle_payload`:
- Changed generated_at_unix_ms in the BundlePayload to a constant 0 so that
  regenerating a bundle from the same source files always produces the same
  hash. This is essential for the verification suite to compare rebuilt
  payloads against committed artifact hashes without requiring exact timestamp
  reproduction.
- Canonicalize both repo_root and individual file paths before computing
  relative paths and reading file content, fixing path resolution issues when
  the repo root contains symlinks.

Integration into the harness:
- `run_all_core_suites` in lib.rs now includes the raptorq artifact suite.
- Added `raptorq_artifact_suite_is_green` unit test.

New `run_raptorq_gate` binary (crates/fnp-conformance/src/bin/run_raptorq_gate.rs):
- Standalone CLI binary that runs the raptorq artifact suite and prints a
  JSON GateSummary with pass/fail status and per-suite case/failure details.
- Exits with code 2 on any failure, suitable for CI gate checks.

New e2e script (scripts/e2e/run_raptorq_gate.sh):
- Shell wrapper that invokes the gate binary via `rch exec`, intended for
  end-to-end CI pipeline integration.

Regenerated artifacts reflect the new deterministic hashing (generated_at_unix_ms
updated, hashes changed from 858961de... to 60dfdde1... for benchmark_bundle_v1
and from de89f767.../a1d12dbd... to new values for conformance_bundle_v1).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -0,0 +1,12 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+set -euo pipefail
+
+ROOT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/../.." && pwd)"
+
+cd "$ROOT_DIR"
+
+echo "[raptorq-gate] root=$ROOT_DIR"
+
+rch exec -- cargo run -p fnp-conformance --bin run_raptorq_gate
+
+echo "[raptorq-gate] completed"
